message:
fix: align in-memory expiry boundaries with inclusive SQL semantics (br-3l1t)

Follow-up to 5bd9a46 which fixed the SQL boundary from `expires_ts < ?`
to `expires_ts <= ?`. This commit aligns all in-memory reservation
expiry filters to use the same inclusive boundary:

- resources.rs: expired filter `< now` → `<= now`, stale filter `>= now` → `> now`
- reservations.rs: force-release expiry check `< now` → `<= now`
- cleanup.rs: stale filter `>= now` → `> now`, updated doc comment
- guard/lib.rs: is_expired() uses `<=` for all 3 parse branches

Also includes rustfmt fixes for queries.rs and messaging.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1bm","title":"HTTP Server Parity: Legacy Python Exact Match","description":"## Objective
Port the **HTTP server stack** to Rust with exact legacy Python behavior: auth, rate limiting, streamable HTTP transport, mail UI endpoints, logging, and background workers.

## Scope
- Middleware: bearer auth, JWT + RBAC, rate limiting.
- Streamable HTTP MCP transport (stateless ASGI adapter behavior, header normalization, base path mounting).
- Mail UI routes and static assets.
- Request logging + OTEL no‑op parity.
- Background workers (ack TTL, file reservation cleanup, tool metrics, retention/quota).
- Health + well‑known endpoints.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests per subsystem (JWT, rate limit, streamable HTTP, mail UI, logging, workers).
- E2E HTTP suite (br-2ei.9.6) covering auth, CORS, streamable, and UI endpoints.

## Logging/Artifacts
- HTTP transcripts + diffs under `tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:32.057076856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646906905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646884493Z","close_reason":"All HTTP server parity child beads complete (auth/JWT/RBAC, rate limit, peer addr/localhost bypass, streamable HTTP, /mail SSR UI, CORS, logging+OTEL no-op, workers, health/well-known)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:48:14.330942304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-36w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:23.374551664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1","title":"JWT Auth Parity (HS256 + JWKS + role claim)","description":"Objective:
Implement JWT authentication exactly as in legacy Python (mcp_agent_mail/http.py). This includes HS256 HMAC and JWKS verification paths, audience/issuer validation, role extraction, and error semantics. Must be 1:1 on outputs and failure modes.

Legacy Reference Behavior (must match):
- If JWT enabled and Authorization missing or not Bearer: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Decode token using:
  - If JWKS URL set: fetch JWKS, select key by header.kid, verify
  - Else if secret set: verify with HMAC secret
  - Algorithms from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default HS256)
- Validate audience/issuer if configured; otherwise ignore
- On any failure: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Extract roles from claim HTTP_JWT_ROLE_CLAIM (default "role")
  - String -> singleton set
  - List/tuple -> set of strings
  - Else -> empty
- If roles empty, use HTTP_RBAC_DEFAULT_ROLE
- Store claims for rate limit identity (sub) and any downstream uses

Implementation Considerations:
- JWKS fetch must be async-safe via asupersync HTTP client (no tokio)
- Cache JWKS by URL with TTL to avoid per-request latency
- Must not change external response schema or status codes
- Log auth failures at debug/trace (no extra HTTP output)

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
Unit tests:
- Missing Authorization header -> 401
- Non-Bearer Authorization -> 401
- HS256 valid/invalid signatures
- JWKS valid/invalid (kid missing, bad signature)
- Audience/issuer mismatch -> 401
- Role claim variants (string, list, missing)
Integration tests:
- Start server with HTTP_JWT_* env vars; make requests with valid/invalid JWT
- Verify RBAC path uses roles correctly
E2E:
- Covered by `br-1bm.1.5` (uses shared harness `br-2ei.9.1`).

Notes:
- Localhost bypass logic is owned by `br-1bm.3` + `br-1bm.8`; JWT must still match legacy in the non-bypass path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JWT behavior matches legacy for all vectors extracted in `br-1bm.1.1`.
- Role extraction matches Python behavior across all supported claim shapes.
- `br-1bm.1.5` unit+integration+E2E suite passes and produces high-signal artifacts on failure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperBarn","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:46.671284098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957613837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957588740Z","close_reason":"All subtasks br-1bm.1.1-1.5 complete; JWT unit+integration+E2E coverage passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1.1","title":"JWT Parity: extract legacy behavior + test vectors","description":"Purpose:
Extract exact legacy JWT behavior and define concrete test vectors so Rust matches Python 1:1.

Legacy Implementation Notes (from http.py):
- Header parse: token.split(".",1)[0]; base64url decode with padding; JSON parse. Any error -> None.
- If jwks_url set:
  - httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5) GET jwks_url -> .json()
  - JsonWebKey.import_key_set(jwks)
  - kid = header.get("kid"); key = find_by_kid(kid) if present else key_set.keys[0]
- Else if secret set:
  - JsonWebKey.import_key(secret, {"kty":"oct"})
- If key None -> Unauthorized.
- jwt.decode(token, key) using algs from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default ["HS256"]). Any error -> Unauthorized.
- If audience configured: claims.validate_aud(audience)
- If issuer configured: if str(claims.get("iss") or "") != issuer -> Unauthorized (no fallback)
- claims.validate() (handles exp/nbf/iat if present)
- Returns dict(claims) on success.

Concrete Test Vectors (must implement):
1) Missing Authorization header -> 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
2) Authorization not Bearer -> 401
3) Header parse fail (malformed base64 / non-JSON header) -> 401
4) JWKS path:
   - jwks_url set, kid present, matching key -> success
   - jwks_url set, kid present, no matching key -> 401
   - jwks_url set, kid missing -> uses first key in key_set
   - jwks fetch fails / invalid JSON -> 401
5) Secret path:
   - jwt_secret set, HS256 valid -> success
   - jwt_secret set, signature invalid -> 401
6) Algorithms:
   - alg not in HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS -> 401
7) Audience:
   - jwt_audience set, token aud matches -> success
   - jwt_audience set, token aud mismatch -> 401
8) Issuer:
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss matches exactly -> success
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss missing or different -> 401
9) Claims validate:
   - exp in past -> 401
   - nbf in future -> 401
   - exp/nbf absent -> ok
10) Role claim:
   - role claim string -> roles={string}
   - role claim list -> roles={strings}
   - role claim missing/invalid -> roles empty -> default role applied downstream

Deliverable:
- A markdown or JSON matrix of inputs -> expected HTTP status/body and roles extracted.
- Must be explicit enough that Rust tests can be derived directly.

Dependencies: none (spec extraction only).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:09.570324403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060998320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060980056Z","close_reason":"Extracted legacy JWT behavior + explicit test vectors in bead description","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1r9v","title":"Console layout config: HUD sizing + persistence","description":"Add interactive console layout configuration allowing users to control HUD size/position vs scrolling log output, with automatic persistence.

USER NEED:
- Configure how much screen real estate the static HUD occupies vs the scrolling log.
  - Examples: bottom 20%, bottom 50%.
- Do this interactively and have settings remembered.

DEFAULTS (must be great out-of-the-box):
- Rich HUD starts automatically in a real TTY when `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` (default).
- Default HUD size is ~33% of terminal height, anchored bottom.

CONFIGURABLE SETTINGS (env vars + persisted user config):
1) `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT`: u8 (10-80, default 33)
   - Maps to: `ui_height = (term_height * pct / 100).max(8).min(term_height - 2)`

2) `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR`: enum {bottom, top} (default bottom)
   - Maps to `ftui::UiAnchor`

3) `CONSOLE_UI_AUTO_SIZE`: bool (default false)
   - When true: use `ScreenMode::InlineAuto { min_height, max_height }` instead of fixed.

4) `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE`: bool (default true)
   - When true, interactive adjustments persist immediately.

PERSISTENCE FILE:
- `~/.config/agent-mail/console.toml`
  - Priority: env vars > console.toml > defaults
  - Saving should preserve stable formatting (don’t churn on every save).

INTERACTIVE ADJUSTMENT:
- HTTP server mode only (stdio mode uses stdin for MCP protocol).
- Spawn a lightweight `/dev/tty` (or stdin) reader thread when running in a real TTY:
  - Up / '+': increase `ui_height_percent` by 5 (max 80)
  - Down / '-': decrease `ui_height_percent` by 5 (min 10)
  - 't'/'b': toggle anchor top/bottom
  - 'a': toggle auto-size
  - 's': force-save settings
  - 'q': disable interactive adjustments for this run
- HUD should display a subtle, dim hint line when interactive mode is active:
  - `+/- resize | t/b anchor | a autosize | s save`

INTEGRATION NOTES:
- Left/right splits ("left 30%") require AltScreen + an internal log viewer.
  - That’s implemented separately in `br-1m6a.19`.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add config fields to `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` + `from_env()` parsing.
- Add load/save for `console.toml` (no new global config system; just this file).
- Modify `StartupDashboard::maybe_start()` to use configured values instead of hardcoded 1/3.

TESTING:
- Config loading priority (env > file > defaults)
- `ui_height` calculation + clamp behavior
- Anchor parsing fallback
- Save/load roundtrip for console.toml
- Interactive key handling logic (unit test the state machine; no real tty required)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Users can resize HUD interactively in HTTP mode and it persists.
- Defaults remain enabled and sane with zero config.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:18:52.502187876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839103222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839080589Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of br-1m6a.19 (merged console layout plan there).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"br-1t0e","title":"Resources: file_reservations resource should be bounded (avoid glob explosion)","description":"## Problem
`resource://file_reservations/{slug}` currently expands glob patterns via filesystem enumeration and may run `git log` over many pathspecs. On large projects with broad patterns (e.g. `src/**`), this can take >60s and effectively wedges the MCP server (subsequent tool/resource calls time out).

## Objective
Make `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` reliably fast and non-blocking for large repos while keeping legacy output shape.

## Scope
- Avoid enumerating all filesystem matches for glob patterns.
- Compute git activity without expanding to thousands of explicit paths (use git pathspec/glob or prefix-based strategy).
- Bound work per pattern (time/size caps) and fail open by returning `null` activity timestamps when exceeding caps.
- Ensure cleanup logic (TTL expiry + stale release) cannot monopolize the server request thread.

## Tests
- Unit tests covering glob-heavy patterns (`src/**`, `crates/**`, `**/*.rs`) ensure the code path does not enumerate full repo trees (use a temp dir with many files to assert completes quickly).
- Regression test: resource handler completes within a small time budget under a synthetic repo with many files and a broad glob reservation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resource://file_reservations/...` returns within a bounded time on large repos (no tool server wedge).
2. Output shape remains compatible (fields present, timestamps may be null when capped).
3. Existing conformance tests still pass; add targeted tests for the new caps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:05.771837055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745259737Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745241343Z","close_reason":"Avoid glob explosion in resource://file_reservations: stop filesystem glob expansion; use git pathspec glob magic; add regression test; ran fmt+clippy+test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1t0e","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:34.586726901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1uf","title":"Share/Export Pipeline Parity (Static Bundle)","description":"## Objective
Implement the full **Share/Export pipeline** (static bundle) as specified in `EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md` (Share/Export section). This includes DB snapshotting, scrub presets, search indexes, viewer assets, bundle scaffolding, signing/encryption, and deterministic ZIP packaging.

## Scope (15 Steps)
1. create_sqlite_snapshot
2. apply_project_scope
3. scrub_snapshot (presets + secret regex)
4. build_search_indexes (FTS5)
5. build_materialized_views
6. create_performance_indexes
7. finalize_snapshot_for_export
8. bundle_attachments
9. maybe_chunk_database
10. copy_viewer_assets
11. export_viewer_data
12. write_bundle_scaffolding
13. sign_manifest
14. encrypt_bundle
15. package_directory_as_zip

## Tests
- Unit tests per step for deterministic behavior.
- Integration tests for end‑to‑end export/update flows.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.4`.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Share pipeline artifacts under `tests/artifacts/share/<timestamp>/`.
- JSON summaries + diffs for each step.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All steps are implemented per spec with legacy parity.
2. Deterministic bundles (stable hashes) are enforced in tests.
3. E2E share/export suite passes with detailed artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:14:52.252531998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.785687923Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.767320259Z","close_reason":"All 15 share/export pipeline steps implemented and covered by deterministic/conformance tests; unblocking downstream CLI/e2e work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1uf","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:14:56.877509340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1uvp","title":"CLI mark-identity integration: default commit path in git repo","description":"Add integration test proving  default behavior commits marker file when run in a git repo. Assert commit subject and marker presence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryBarn","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:56:47.301428435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:57:17.562423583Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T19:57:17.562395751Z","close_reason":"Completed: added default-commit mark-identity integration test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-21gj","title":"Dual-mode interface program: MCP-first default + opt-in agent-first CLI","description":"Background:
Users asked for an explicit dual-mode interface strategy:
1) MCP mode must remain the default.
2) A separate setting (OFF by default) can disable MCP-first behavior and enable a full agent-first CLI command surface.
3) When MCP mode is ON, CLI invocation should fail fast with a clear message like "Agent Mail is not a CLI!" and actionable guidance.

Why this matters:
The current surface area is confusing across binaries and command families. We need one coherent contract so operators, agents, and docs all align.

Outcome:
A production-ready dual-mode architecture with explicit guardrails, clear UX, test coverage, and rollout playbook.

Program-level definition of done:
- Mode contract is documented and enforced.
- MCP default remains unchanged.
- CLI mode is explicit opt-in.
- Denial path in MCP mode is deterministic and tested.
- CLI mode command parity roadmap is implemented and validated.","design":"Dual-mode interface is implemented as a single authoritative mode decision (resolver) consumed by all entrypoints. Mode controls command dispatch policy, UX messaging, and allowed command families. MCP mode remains default and is conservative by design; CLI mode is explicit opt-in via config switch.","acceptance_criteria":"1) MCP mode is default and denies CLI command execution with canonical message/hints. 2) CLI mode opt-in enables documented command families with agent-first ergonomics. 3) Behavior is validated by unit/integration/conformance/perf tests. 4) Docs and rollout playbooks are updated.","notes":"Planning assumptions: no implicit mode auto-switching; no hidden compatibility shims; no mode decisions duplicated in per-command handlers. This epic intentionally front-loads policy/contract work to avoid implementation drift.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:42.620422362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:58:15.530985604Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:58:15.530859328Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"br-21gj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution blueprint:\n1) Finish Track 1 policy artifacts first (contract, denial UX, allowlist, parity matrix).\n2) Build Track 2 resolver/config foundation.\n3) Implement Track 3 guardrails and Track 4 command families in parallel where dependencies permit.\n4) Run Track 5 validation suites as hard gate.\n5) Complete Track 6 docs/rollout only after quality gates pass.\n\nParallelization guidance:\n- Track 1 tasks can be split among spec owners but must converge before Track 3/4 starts.\n- Track 4 command families (4.2-4.7) are parallelizable after 4.1.\n\nRisk notes:\n- Primary risk is accidental CLI execution in MCP mode; treat denial path as security boundary.\n- Secondary risk is parity drift; close via 4.8 + 5.3 conformance checks.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:19:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-27m","title":"Share: search indexes + materialized views + perf indexes","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 4–6: FTS5 search indexes, materialized views, and performance indexes for static viewer.

## Scope
- **build_search_indexes**: create `fts_messages` virtual table; populate with subject/body/importance/project/thread_key/created_ts; optimize.
- **build_materialized_views**: create `message_overview_mv`, `attachments_by_message_mv`, `fts_search_overview_mv` (if FTS enabled).
- **create_performance_indexes**: add lowercase columns and covering indexes for fast viewer filtering.

## Tests
- Unit tests for FTS presence/absence path.
- Integration tests verifying view row counts and index existence.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store schema dumps under `tests/artifacts/share/indexes/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. FTS and MV tables match legacy schemas and row counts.
2. Performance indexes created without errors, idempotent.
3. - CORS preflight (br-33ha.4.4) — 12+ assertions

- Concurrent agents (br-33ha.6.2) — 12+ assertions

- JSON summary output (br-33ha.7.2) — CI integration [blocked by .7.1]
- Tool metrics E2E (br-33ha.7.3) — 10+ assertions

- ~85+ new E2E assertions across 6 new test scripts
- Logging improvements across ~28 existing suites
- JSON CI report infrastructure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:14:19.386663950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:39:54.749574466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:39:54.749559628Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"br-3np","title":"CLI: share export/update parity (interactive + defaults)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share export` and `share update` commands with legacy behavior (including `--interactive`).

## Scope
- Flags: `--output/-o`, `--interactive/-i`, `--project/-p` (repeat), thresholds, scrub preset, chunk settings, `--dry-run`, `--zip`, `--signing-key`, `--signing-public-out`, `--age-recipient` (repeat).
- Defaults: inline=64KiB, detach=25MiB, chunk threshold=20MiB, chunk size=4MiB; scrub preset `standard`.
- Validation: invalid preset exits 1; enforce chunk size >=1024; auto‑adjust detach threshold if <= inline.
- `share export`:
  - `--dry-run` creates temp output + prints summary only.
  - `--interactive` wizard prompts and overrides flags.
- `share update`:
  - loads config from existing `manifest.json` and stored export config.
  - default `--zip` is **false** (contrast export default true).

## Tests
- Unit tests for flag parsing + defaults.
- Integration tests with temp bundle export/update; verify manifest config updates.
- Golden output for `--dry-run` summary.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store CLI outputs under `tests/artifacts/cli/share/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share export/update match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. interactive mode overrides flags and produces identical outputs to legacy.
3. dry-run produces no bundle artifacts but prints expected summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:51.526681473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:02:13.427048743Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:02:13.427025710Z","close_reason":"Implemented share export/update parity (interactive wizard + defaults + dry-run summary + update sync + signing/zip/age warnings) and verified via cargo test gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3np","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:33.896970708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3np","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:56.127446696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"br-3np","author":"PearlOwl","text":"Progress: implemented  wizard + real  summary; ensured export pipeline copies viewer assets; fixed export snapshot finalization to rebuild  when schema mismatched and to drop legacy FTS triggers so message updates don’t fail.  {"id":"br-3op","title":"Share: bundle attachments (inline/detach/external/missing)","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Step 8: attachment bundling and classification.

## Scope
- Thresholds: inline <= 64KiB, detach >= 25MiB (defaults, override via CLI).
- Classify attachments into inline (embed), detached (file), external (URL), missing.
- Generate attachment manifest entries with hashes and metadata.
- Honor `convert_images` and attachment embed policy.

## Tests
- Unit tests for threshold logic and classification.
- Integration tests with sample attachments (inline, large, external, missing).

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store attachment manifests and hashes under `tests/artifacts/share/attachments/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Attachment classification matches legacy thresholds and rules.
2. Manifests include correct hashes and metadata.
3. Missing/external attachments are handled gracefully with clear markers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:56.123691848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:56.435100382Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:56.435066689Z","close_reason":"Attachment bundling/classification already implemented in bundle.rs; bundle_* tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3op","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:02.590128847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-9jl","title":"CLI: share verify/decrypt parity (signature + age)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share verify` and `share decrypt` with legacy semantics.

## Scope
- `share verify`:
  - Validate SRI hashes and optional Ed25519 signature.
  - `--public-key` uses provided base64 key; else use manifest signature.
- `share decrypt`:
  - `--output` defaults to filename sans `.age` (or `_decrypted`).
  - `--identity` (age key file) is mutually exclusive with `--passphrase`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and decrypt flag validation.
- Integration tests decrypting a bundle produced by share export.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store verification results under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_verify/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share verify/decrypt match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. Signature validation and age decrypt succeed on valid inputs and fail loudly on invalid.
3. Mutual‑exclusion flag handling matches legacy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:17.420980860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917640762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917621787Z","close_reason":"Added CLI integration tests for share verify/decrypt parity; validated exit codes + defaults","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:44.284948962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:23.973796081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ave","title":"Share: sqlite snapshot + project scope","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 1–2: `create_sqlite_snapshot` and `apply_project_scope` with exact legacy semantics.

## Scope
- **create_sqlite_snapshot**:
  - Resolve destination path; create parent dirs.
  - Error if destination exists (never overwrite).
  - Open source DB; run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` when enabled.
  - Use sqlite backup API to copy source → destination.
- **apply_project_scope**:
  - Load projects (id, slug, human_key); error if none.
  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.
  - Delete order per spec (agent_links, sibling_suggestions, messages + recipients, file_reservations, agents, projects).

## Tests
- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.
- Integration tests with temp DB and multiple projects; verify deletions and counts.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. Tests are deterministic and isolated to temp DB paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:05.665286231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363640110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363620834Z","close_reason":"Implemented backup snapshot + scope parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ave","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:11.619634150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-hk3v","title":"Dev UX: am runner script + /api<->/mcp HTTP path alias","description":"## Problem
Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the alias path logic (path_allowed + canonicalization).

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/am serve works without manual exports in a typical dev setup (token file present).
- With default config (HTTP_PATH unset), both POST /api/ and POST /mcp/ reach the MCP handler.
- Full quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:31:23.230475657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:50:46.201686047Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:50:46.201652053Z","close_reason":"Completed: scripts/am added, /api<->/mcp aliasing wired + tests, README updated, quality gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
